hero.headline: LPU Online MBA in Logistics and Supply Chain Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: Lovely Professional University's UGC-DEB approved online MBA with a Logistics and Supply Chain Management specialisation. NIRF #31 University, NAAC A+. Rs 2,00,000 standard fee, Rs 1,61,600 with 20% Student Grant. Zero-cost 24-month EMI at Rs 6,733/month. Supply Chain Strategy, Warehouse Management, International Logistics, Transportation Management, and Procurement in Sem 3-4.

sections.tldr: LPU Online MBA Logistics and SCM: NIRF #31, NAAC A+, UGC-DEB approved, Rs 2L standard or Rs 1.61L with 20% grant, zero-cost 24-month EMI. SCM depth with Supply Chain Strategy, Warehouse Management, International Logistics, Transportation Management, Procurement and Inventory Management, and Supply Chain Analytics. Strong for logistics manager, supply chain manager, and procurement careers.

about.body:
The LPU Online MBA in Logistics and Supply Chain Management is offered by Lovely Professional University's online division. LPU holds NAAC A+ accreditation, NIRF #31 University ranking (2025), and NIRF #44 in Management. The online programme is UGC-DEB approved.

The Logistics and Supply Chain Management specialisation delivers dedicated Sem 3-4 subjects covering Supply Chain Strategy, Warehouse Management, International Logistics, Transportation Management, Procurement and Inventory Management, and Supply Chain Analytics. These subjects cover the full logistics and supply chain function from warehouse operations to global freight and procurement strategy.

LPU's Punjab base is strategically located near major logistics hubs including Ludhiana's manufacturing belt, Chandigarh's industrial zone, and North India's road freight corridors. The zero-cost EMI structure makes LPU Logistics and SCM MBA the most affordable NIRF-ranked supply chain management credential for working logistics professionals.

sem3Subjects.name: Supply Chain Strategy
sem3Subjects.description: Design supply chain network strategy: sourcing footprint, make-vs-buy decisions, and long-range supply chain transformation roadmaps.

sem3Subjects.name: Warehouse Management
sem3Subjects.description: Design warehouse layout, manage WMS implementation, and optimise pick-pack-ship operations for e-commerce and manufacturing.

sem3Subjects.name: Transportation Management
sem3Subjects.description: Optimise freight modes, manage 3PL relationships, negotiate carrier contracts, and design last-mile delivery networks.

sem3Subjects.name: Corporate Strategy and Entrepreneurship
sem3Subjects.description: Apply logistics entrepreneurship, 3PL startup strategy, and supply chain innovation to competitive business strategy.

sem3Subjects.name: Generic Elective I
sem3Subjects.description: Elective subject from the GE basket chosen by the student to complement the Logistics and SCM specialisation.

sem3Subjects.name: Seminar on Summer Training
sem3Subjects.description: Present logistics and supply chain summer training findings to faculty panel, developing supply chain communication skills.

sem4Subjects.name: Capstone Project
sem4Subjects.description: Complete an applied logistics project on supply chain optimisation, 3PL network design, or inventory management strategy.

sem4Subjects.name: International Logistics
sem4Subjects.description: Manage cross-border freight forwarding, customs clearance, incoterms application, and international distribution network design.

sem4Subjects.name: Procurement and Inventory Management
sem4Subjects.description: Design procurement category strategies, manage vendor evaluation, and apply EOQ, JIT, and ABC analysis for inventory optimisation.

sem4Subjects.name: Supply Chain Analytics
sem4Subjects.description: Apply demand forecasting models, inventory analytics, and supply chain performance dashboards for data-driven SCM decisions.

sem4Subjects.name: Generic Elective II
sem4Subjects.description: Elective subject from the GE basket chosen by the student to complement the Logistics and SCM specialisation.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 shared core subjects across all LPU Online MBA specialisations. See the main LPU Online MBA page for core subject list.

whoHires.body:
Logistics and Supply Chain Management MBA graduates from LPU Online are hired across these industries and roles:

**Industries:** E-commerce and quick-commerce logistics, FMCG supply chain, Manufacturing, 3PL and freight forwarding, Retail distribution, Pharma supply chain

**Entry-level roles:** Supply Chain Analyst, Logistics Coordinator, Procurement Executive, Warehouse Manager, 3PL Coordinator

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Supply Chain Manager, Head of Logistics, Procurement Manager, Warehouse Operations Manager, Distribution Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** VP Supply Chain, COO, Head of Procurement, Supply Chain Transformation Director

**Top hiring organisations:** Amazon India Logistics, Flipkart Supply Chain, Delhivery, Blue Dart, TCI Express, Mahindra Logistics, DHL India, Maersk India, HUL Supply Chain, Nestle India Supply Chain, Lupin Pharma Logistics, Hero MotoCorp Procurement

skills.body:
The LPU Logistics and SCM specialisation builds skills that logistics and supply chain employers test at SCM manager and procurement manager levels:

**Technical skills:**
- Supply Chain Strategy: network design, sourcing footprint planning, make-vs-buy analysis, and supply chain transformation roadmap development for strategic SCM roles
- Procurement and Inventory Management: category strategy design, vendor evaluation, EOQ and ABC analysis, and JIT inventory management for procurement and purchasing roles
- Supply Chain Analytics: demand forecasting models, inventory optimisation dashboards, and supply chain KPI reporting for data-driven SCM management
- International Logistics: freight forwarding management, customs clearance, incoterms application, and international distribution network design for global logistics roles

**Applied skills:**
- Warehouse Management: WMS implementation, warehouse layout design, and pick-pack-ship operations optimisation for e-commerce and manufacturing warehouse management
- Transportation Management: freight mode optimisation, 3PL contract negotiation, and last-mile delivery network design for logistics operations

**Soft skills:** Presenting supply chain analytics to COO-level management, negotiating 3PL and carrier contracts, building business cases for logistics technology investment, and managing cross-functional supply chain transformation projects.

comparisons.body:
Comparing LPU Online MBA Logistics and Supply Chain Management against peers:

**vs SSODL Online MBA Logistics and Supply Chain Management:** SSODL (Symbiosis) is NAAC A++ and NIRF #24 at Rs 3,15,000-3,70,000 with interest-bearing EMI. SSODL adds dedicated Sustainable Supply Chain and Technology in Supply Chain subjects. For Pune and Maharashtra manufacturing supply chain careers, SSODL carries stronger brand recognition. For North India logistics careers at significantly lower fees with zero-cost EMI, LPU is the accessible alternative.

**vs MUJ Online MBA Supply Chain Management:** MUJ is NAAC A+ and NIRF #58 at Rs 1,53,000-1,80,000 with Coursera Plus. MUJ Supply Chain includes global certification content. LPU Logistics and SCM includes dedicated Supply Chain Analytics and Transportation Management as distinct subjects. Both are comparable at similar fee levels. LPU adds zero-cost EMI advantage.

**vs Amity Online MBA Production and Operations Management:** Amity Production and Operations covers supply chain as part of broader operations management. LPU Logistics and SCM dedicates all Sem 3-4 subjects to supply chain and logistics specifics including Warehouse Management, International Logistics, and Supply Chain Analytics. For specialised logistics careers, LPU is more targeted, and more affordable.

fees.body:
The LPU Online MBA in Logistics and Supply Chain Management offers 2 fee tracks:

**Track 1 - Standard Fee**:
- Registration fee: Rs 1,000 (one-time)
- Total program fee: Rs 2,00,000
- Semester fee: Rs 50,000 per semester across 4 semesters

**Track 2 - With 20% Student Grant**:
- Registration fee: Rs 1,000 (one-time)
- Total program fee: Rs 1,61,600 after 20% Student Grant
- Semester fee: Rs 40,400 per semester

**Zero-Cost EMI option (Track 2)**:
- 24-month zero-cost EMI: Rs 6,733 per month
- No interest charged. No processing fee from the university.
- EMI is routed through the university's marketing partner.

The 20% Student Grant is applied at admission. Confirm current grant eligibility and applicable conditions with our counsellor before applying.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with LPU each quarter. Please reconfirm current fees and grant eligibility with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body: The degree certificate is issued by Lovely Professional University (LPU) and carries the LPU name with the MBA designation. The Logistics and Supply Chain Management specialisation is noted on the marksheet and transcript. Online mode is identified per UGC-DEB 2020 regulations. The certificate is valid for corporate hiring, PSU eligibility, and higher education admission across India.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from LPU Online MBA Logistics and Supply Chain Management alumni. Ratings from post-completion surveys.

items.name: Kulwinder Singh
items.liked: Supply Chain Strategy and Procurement and Inventory Management were directly applicable to my manufacturing supply chain role. Zero-cost EMI was the deciding factor.
items.disliked: Supply Chain Analytics could benefit from more Python and Excel-based exercises.
items.body: I had 5 years in logistics operations and the LPU SCM MBA helped me transition to supply chain manager. The Rs 1.61L with zero-cost EMI was unbeatable. LPU brand is well recognised at North India manufacturing and logistics companies.

items.name: Radhika Patel
items.liked: Warehouse Management and International Logistics were excellent and practically applicable. Strong for e-commerce operations roles.
items.disliked: Transportation Management could include more quick-commerce last-mile delivery case studies.
items.body: Good programme for supply chain career advancement. Delhi-NCR logistics companies recognised the LPU credential. The zero-cost EMI made it accessible compared to SSODL.

items.name: Sukhchain Mann
items.liked: International Logistics and Supply Chain Analytics subjects were strong. Good foundation for global logistics roles.
items.disliked: Limited case study coverage of pharma cold chain and temperature-controlled logistics.
items.body: Strong logistics and SCM content at an affordable price. For North India manufacturing and 3PL careers, LPU is a recognised and cost-effective choice. Zero-cost EMI was a genuine benefit.

faqs.question: How does the LPU 20% Student Grant work?
faqs.answer: The 20% Student Grant reduces the standard Rs 2,00,000 fee to Rs 1,61,600. It is applied at admission. Confirm current grant eligibility conditions with our counsellor before applying, as grant availability may vary by intake cycle.

faqs.question: Is LPU Online MBA zero-cost EMI genuinely interest-free?
faqs.answer: Yes. The 24-month zero-cost EMI at Rs 6,733 per month carries no interest charge and no processing fee from the university. It is routed through the university's marketing partner. Confirm current EMI terms before committing.

faqs.question: Is LPU Logistics MBA suitable for e-commerce operations professionals?
faqs.answer: Yes. Warehouse Management, Transportation Management, and International Logistics subjects are directly applicable to e-commerce fulfilment and quick-commerce operations roles. Supply Chain Analytics provides the data skills needed for e-commerce supply chain optimisation.

faqs.question: Does LPU Logistics MBA cover procurement and vendor management?
faqs.answer: Yes. Procurement and Inventory Management is a dedicated Sem 4 subject covering category strategy, vendor evaluation frameworks, EOQ and ABC inventory analysis, and JIT implementation. This is directly applicable to procurement manager and category manager roles.

faqs.question: Is LPU Online MBA NIRF ranked?
faqs.answer: Yes. Lovely Professional University is ranked NIRF #31 (University category, 2025) and NIRF #44 in the Management category. This places LPU among the top-ranked universities offering UGC-DEB approved online MBAs in India.

chooseIf: You are in logistics, supply chain, procurement, or warehouse management roles and want an affordable NIRF-ranked MBA.
You want a UGC-DEB approved MBA from a NIRF #31 university at Rs 1,61,600 (with grant) or Rs 2,00,000 standard.
You want 24-month zero-cost EMI and dedicated Supply Chain Analytics and International Logistics coverage.
consider: If Sustainable Supply Chain and Technology in Supply Chain depth is required, SSODL Online MBA Logistics and SCM offers more specialised coverage at Rs 3.15L.
If Manipal brand with Coursera Plus global supply chain certifications is preferred, MUJ Online MBA Supply Chain Management is available at comparable fees.
If analytics-led supply chain with quantitative modelling is the focus, NMIMS Online MBA Operations and Data Sciences Management offers deeper analytics depth.
